title: pterocarpus micropterus
intro: Pterocarpus micropterus, also known as the African Bloodwood or Red Sandalwood, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to have various health benefits including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's heartwood and root bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been shown to have antiseptic and antifungal properties, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
